assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows mixed results: 2.1.x versions marked YES (affected) and 2.2.x versions marked NO (not affected) based on lock file evidence
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 9-15: Table shows 2.1.0 (h2 0.4.5, YES), 2.1.1 (h2 0.4.5, YES), 2.2.0 (h2 0.4.8, NO), 2.2.1 (h2 0.4.8, NO), 2.2.2 (retag, NO), 2.2.3 (h2 0.4.9, NO), 2.2.4 (h2 0.4.9, NO). Determinations are based on lock file h2 version comparisons against the fix threshold of 0.4.8.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction includes only the actually affected versions (2.1.x versions), not the unaffected 2.2.x versions (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md lines 36-38: 'Current: [RHTPA 2.1.0, RHTPA 2.2.0]' corrected to 'Proposed: [RHTPA 2.1.0, RHTPA 2.1.1]'. RHTPA 2.2.0 is explicitly removed ('h2 0.4.8 >= 0.4.8 -- not affected') and RHTPA 2.1.1 is added. Only 2.1.x versions remain in the proposed Affects Versions.

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks are created only for the affected stream (2.1.x), not for 2.2.x which already ships the patched version (Step 7 Case A scoping)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 4-9: 'Case A: Affected -- the 2.1.x stream ships vulnerable h2 versions. Remediation tasks are required for the 2.1.x stream only. The 2.2.x stream is not affected (ships h2 >= 0.4.8). No remediation tasks are created for the 2.2.x stream.' Two tasks created targeting release/0.3.z (2.1.x stream) only. Lines 160-166 explicitly explain why no 2.2.x remediation is needed.

assertion_results.text: The analysis treats the issue as unscoped (no stream suffix) and checks ALL versions across all streams (Step 1 Stream scope resolution, unscoped path)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md line 13: 'Product Version (PSIRT-claimed): (none — no stream suffix in summary; issue is unscoped)'. Lines 37-38: 'The issue summary has no stream suffix in brackets. This is an unscoped issue covering all configured version streams. Steps 2-7 will analyze all streams.' Lines 42-43 list both 2.1.x and 2.2.x streams for analysis. The version impact table in version-impact.md covers all 7 versions across both streams.

assertion_results.text: Cross-stream impact notice is NOT generated because the issue is unscoped — it covers all streams by definition (Step 7 Case B applies only to scoped issues)
assertion_results.evidence: No cross-stream impact notice section or file exists in the outputs. A grep for 'cross-stream', 'Cross-stream', and 'Case B' across all output files returned only one hit: version-impact.md line 27 ('mixed impact across streams') which is a factual observation in the version impact analysis, not a dedicated cross-stream impact notice. The remediation.md file contains no cross-stream notice section. The issue is explicitly identified as unscoped (data-extraction.md line 37), so Case B (which applies only to scoped issues) does not trigger.
